body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
irrlicht_observes: no

## Re-evaluation (2026-05-23)

This is a re-assessment of the 2026-05-18 verdict (yes/no, conf 0.85). The verdict is unchanged. The two questions for the re-eval:

1. **Does the agent still ship the feature?** Yes — confirmed against the live `claude` binary (v2.1.150 at `~/.local/share/claude/versions/2.1.150`). A `strings` scan shows the `run_in_background` Bash-tool parameter ("Set to true to run this command in the background"), the `backgroundTaskId` output field (34 hits), and the companion `BashOutput` (14 hits) and `KillShell` (8 hits) tools. The agent surface is real and documented in the tool descriptions baked into the binary.

2. **Has irrlicht added background-process tracking since 2026-05-18?** No. A grep across the entire `core/` tree for `BackgroundProcessCount`, `BackgroundProcess`, `run_in_background`, `BashOutput`, `KillShell`, `bash_id`, `BackgroundProcessTracker`, and `BackgroundProcessCounter` returned **zero matches** (exit 1). A second grep scoped to `core/domain`, `core/ports`, and `core/application` was also empty. Nothing landed.

## Why irrlicht still can't observe it

irrlicht's transport for claudecode is `FilesUnderRoot{transcriptsDir(), JSONLineParser}` (agent.go:36) with `Process.Match = ExactName{Name: ProcessName}` (agent.go:33). A backgrounded shell process spawned by `Bash run_in_background: true` is a raw child of the `claude` parent — it has no transcript of its own, and the `ExactName{"claude"}` matcher binds only the parent binary, never the shell child, as a session.

The parser confirms the gap concretely. `collectToolUse` (parser.go:338) records every tool_use's `id`/`name` into `ev.ToolUses`, but only special-cases the *inputs* of `TaskCreate`, `TaskUpdate`, and `AskUserQuestion` (parser.go:348-375). It never reads a `Bash` tool's input, so the `run_in_background` flag and the `backgroundTaskId` it returns are never extracted. `resolveAssistantStreaming` (parser.go:288) treats `stop_reason: end_turn` as terminal, so the moment the Bash tool_use returns (immediately, for a backgrounded command) the turn closes and `IsAgentDone()` takes the session to `ready` — regardless of whether the spawned process is still alive.

Mapped to the spec's User-observable signal bullets — what no event in `events.jsonl` would prove today:

- `BackgroundProcessCount > 0 while any background process is alive` — there is no `BackgroundProcessCount` field on `SessionMetrics` or `ParsedEvent` anywhere in the tree (grep empty). The capability isn't even declared as a key in `replaydata/agents/claudecode/capabilities.json`.
- `agent stays working until the last background process exits` — moot: the parent transcript emits `end_turn` as soon as the Bash tool returns, so the session never enters the extended `working` window.
- `then → ready` — happens, but for the wrong reason (normal turn-end), not because the daemon observed the background process exit.

## What would unblock it

Unchanged from 2026-05-18, and still non-trivial. A parser extension would need to (a) snapshot the `backgroundTaskId` from a `Bash` tool_use whose input carries `run_in_background: true`, (b) maintain a per-session `BackgroundProcessCount` that increments on spawn and decrements on a `BashOutput` tool_result reporting a terminated status (or a `KillShell` tool_use), and (c) hold the session in `working` while the count > 0 via a `SubagentCounter`-style hook. The hard part is liveness: Claude Code only surfaces background-process exit on demand when the agent/user polls via `BashOutput` — there is no asynchronous exit event the JSONL tailer can observe. A reliable signal would require either an upstream hook (none exists) or process-tree walking from the `claude` PID. This remains a candidate follow-up issue, not in-scope coverage.
caveats: agent_supports is yes — confirmed against the live claude v2.1.150 binary: run_in_background Bash parameter + BashOutput + KillShell + backgroundTaskId all present in the strings scan.

irrlicht_observes is no because nothing changed since 2026-05-18: (1) a core/-wide grep for BackgroundProcessCount / BackgroundProcess / run_in_background / BashOutput / KillShell / bash_id / BackgroundProcessTracker / BackgroundProcessCounter is empty, (2) the parser's collectToolUse introspects only TaskCreate/TaskUpdate/AskUserQuestion inputs — never Bash, (3) the transport is FilesUnderRoot + ExactName{claude}, so the shell child has no transcript and no PID binding, and (4) there is no asynchronous liveness signal — Claude Code reports background-process exit only on demand via BashOutput.

feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant does NOT apply here — this is a hard transport gap, not a narrow detail. There is no BackgroundProcessCount observable at all, so the verdict is a clean irrlicht_observes: no rather than partial.

Recording the recipe is not blocked but would document the gap, not validate the spec: a fixture prompting Claude to launch a Bash run_in_background process would show the (current) behavior of the session going ready while the process is still alive.

sources.note: Source = FilesUnderRoot{transcriptsDir(), JSONLineParser} (line 36); Process.Match = ExactName{Name: ProcessName} (line 33). Only the parent claude binary is a session; backgrounded Bash children are never matched as sessions.

collectToolUse (line 338) records tool_use id/name but only introspects inputs for TaskCreate/TaskUpdate/AskUserQuestion (lines 348-375) — never Bash, so run_in_background is unread. resolveAssistantStreaming (line 288) lets end_turn fire as soon as the Bash tool_use returns, taking the session to ready regardless of background-process liveness. No BackgroundProcessCount field exists on ParsedEvent.
No background_process / background-process capability key is declared — the primitive isn't even modeled as a feature flag for this adapter.

Re-eval evidence (2026-05-23): zero matches across the entire core/ tree (exit 1). A second grep scoped to core/domain core/ports core/application was also empty. Confirms no background-process transport landed since the 2026-05-18 assessment.
Confirms agent_supports: yes — claude v2.1.150 ships run_in_background (Bash param), backgroundTaskId (34 hits), BashOutput (14 hits), KillShell (8 hits).
